description: Offline-first gameplay relationship graph connecting DataByteSprites systems, source owners, sprite species, lore records, assets, and future runtime integrations.

graphModel.direction: Edges generally read as source edgeType target. For example, capture-flow dependsOn species-index, species-index feeds DataByteDex.

nodes.label: Scanner Shell
nodes.description: Permanent Scanner OS home layer for discovery and encounter flow.

nodes.label: Encounter Discovery
nodes.description: Signal-based discovery loop that finds wild sprites and starts encounter flow.

nodes.label: Capture Flow
nodes.description: Capture roll, DataByte Coin use, and success/failure path.

nodes.label: Battle System
nodes.description: Attack/subdue/reward loop for sprite encounters.

nodes.label: DataByteDex Progress
nodes.description: Collection and completion tracking foundation.

nodes.label: Party Management
nodes.description: Party slots and lead sprite system awaiting battle integration.

nodes.label: XP Rewards
nodes.description: Reward and XP backend with missing Scanner UI display.

nodes.label: Progression and Scanner Rank
nodes.description: Rank and progression layer for scanner/admin growth.

nodes.label: Journal
nodes.description: Future journal/log system for discovery history and lore notes.

nodes.label: Missions
nodes.description: Future scanner objectives and progression tasks.

nodes.label: Sprite Species Index
nodes.description: Species metadata, rarity, evolution lines, capture hooks, and battle stat seeds.

nodes.label: Lore Index
nodes.description: Story canon, characters, artifacts, events, rules, and timeline foundations.

nodes.label: Source Files Index
nodes.description: Active runtime owners, legacy references, patch layers, and future rebuild targets.

nodes.label: databyte-standalone-app.js
nodes.description: Core Scanner OS state and rendering owner.

nodes.label: dd-battle-os-v2.js
nodes.description: Current battle flow owner.

nodes.label: dd-dex-progress.js
nodes.description: Dex progress UI owner.

nodes.label: Leovolt
nodes.description: Rogue starter and first-capture sprite.

nodes.label: Screensavior Line
nodes.description: Screensavior to Monitor Man screen-guardian line.

nodes.label: DisplayDevil Line
nodes.description: DisplayDevil to Mirror Master reflection/trickster line.

nodes.label: NullKing
nodes.description: Locked Null-aligned future boss/threat seed.

nodes.label: Null Signal Origin
nodes.description: Inciting event that causes sprites to manifest.

nodes.label: DataByte Coin
nodes.description: Capture artifact that traps and stabilizes sprites.

nodes.label: First Capture
nodes.description: Vic captures Leovolt, proving DataByteSprites can be studied.

nodes.label: Screens and Signal Manifestation
nodes.description: Screens and signal anomalies act as manifestation gateways.

nodes.label: DataByteDex Renderer Integration
nodes.description: Future renderer that consumes species and lore indexes for DataByteDex pages.

nodes.label: Capture Pool Integration
nodes.description: Future runtime capture pool generated from species metadata.

nodes.label: Battle Balance Pass
nodes.description: Future stat balancing pass using species battle stat seeds.

edges.reason: Scanner OS shell state and rendering live in the standalone app runtime owner.
Discovery flow starts from the permanent scanner shell.
Future encounter pools should draw from species metadata.
Discovery rules are grounded in signal and screen manifestation lore.
Capture mechanics are explained by the DataByte Coin artifact.
Capture difficulty and encounter behavior should be driven by species records.
Capture pools will be generated from rarity, discovery, and capture fields.
The capture runtime should consume generated pool metadata.
Battle flow ownership is tracked in dd-battle-os-v2.js.
Battle stats and typing should come from species records.
Placeholder battle stats must be tuned before runtime combat uses them as final data.
Balanced stats will feed battle calculations.
Party Slot 1 should connect into real battle calculations.
Battle outcomes feed XP and reward progression.
Capture results also generate rewards and progress.
Scanner rank should advance from XP/reward data.
Dex progress UI has a dedicated runtime owner.
Dex completion should be generated from species entries.
DataByteDex should show lore hooks for species, events, artifacts, and rules.
Species records become DataByteDex source content.
Lore records enrich DataByteDex entries.
Dex progress should connect to rendered DataByteDex content.
Leovolt is the first major sprite tied to the Null signal outbreak.
The first capture event centers on Leovolt.
The DataByte Coin enables the first capture.
Screen manifestation rules support the screen-guardian line.
Screen manifestation rules support the reflection/trickster line.
NullKing belongs to deeper Null corruption lore.
Journal entries should be generated from lore and timeline records.
Missions can be seeded from lore events and timeline beats.
Source-file ownership documents which file owns the scanner.
Source-file ownership documents battle runtime ownership.
Source-file ownership documents Dex progress runtime ownership.

highestRiskGaps.summary: Party management exists but still needs real battle integration.
Species metadata exists but is not yet consumed by capture, battle, or DataByteDex runtime.
Lore data exists but is not yet surfaced through journal, missions, or DataByteDex rendering.
